rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for binding assignments.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when generating AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should define interface behavior using visible ports and features.
Implementation contracts should bind to subcomponents and connections using valid implementation scope identifiers.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must reference valid AADL component ports and features.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-specific bindings in component type scopes.
Do not assume that AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: Type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
